join_order.notes: No separate dim_filter phase. During orders scan, check customer_custkey_lookup[o_custkey] -> row_idx, then c_mktsegment[row_idx] == building_code. Lookup is 5.7MB + mktsegment 1.4MB = 7.1MB, fits in LLC (44MB). Eliminates 9ms single-threaded bitset build entirely.
Use lineitem_orderkey_index for contiguous range lookups per qualifying order

deferred_columns.name: o_shippriority
deferred_columns.note: Late materialized — only looked up for final 10 result rows during output phase. Do NOT read during main scan.

scan.io_method: pread_parallel — allocate 3 aligned buffers (posix_memalign, 4096-aligned) for o_orderdate, o_custkey, o_orderkey. In the main parallel region BEFORE the scan loop, each thread does pread() for its morsel range of each column. This avoids mmap minor-fault overhead and mmap_sem kernel lock contention with 64 threads. Total: 171MB from page cache.

data_structures.customer_filter: NONE — no bitset, no separate phase. Use customer_custkey_lookup index + c_mktsegment column inline during orders scan.
data_structures.aggregation: NONE — o_orderkey is PK so each qualifying order produces exactly one complete group. Compute revenue sum inline from lineitem rows via index.
data_structures.topk: per-thread stack-allocated array of 10 ResultRow structs. ResultRow stores {double revenue, int32_t orderkey, int32_t orderdate, int32_t order_row_idx}. Note: store order_row_idx instead of shippriority — look up o_shippriority[order_row_idx] only for final 10 results in output phase (late materialization).

parallelism.notes: Single #pragma omp parallel region for everything. Phase 1: parallel pread of orders columns into aligned buffers (each thread reads its range). Phase 2: parallel scan orders + inline customer check + probe lineitem + top-10. No separate dim_filter phase. No OMP warmup region. No #pragma omp barrier needed — pread phase uses #pragma omp for (implicit barrier at end), then scan phase uses #pragma omp for.

indexes.purpose: inline customer membership check: lookup[o_custkey] -> customer_row_idx, then c_mktsegment[customer_row_idx] == building_code
contiguous range lookup for lineitem rows per qualifying orderkey

optimization_notes: Category A+C — Plan restructuring + work elimination. Three root causes addressed: (1) dim_filter was a separate single-threaded phase taking 9ms to build a customer bitset; eliminated entirely by checking customer membership inline during the orders scan via customer_custkey_lookup[o_custkey] -> c_mktsegment[row_idx] (7.1MB total, fits LLC). (2) 57MB of o_shippriority was read during the full orders scan but only 10 values are ever used; deferred to output phase via late materialization (store order_row_idx in heap, look up shippriority for final 10 rows only). (3) advise_random() on lineitem columns disabled OS readahead, but access is forward-sequential since orders is sorted by o_orderkey and lineitem by l_orderkey — probes advance monotonically; removing MADV_RANDOM allows hardware prefetching to work. Additionally, orders columns are loaded via parallel pread into user-space buffers instead of mmap to avoid kernel mmap_sem contention with 64 threads triggering minor page faults simultaneously. init_date_tables() is deferred to output phase since it is only used for date string formatting in CSV output. IMPORTANT: Do NOT use madvise(MADV_WILLNEED) or madvise(MADV_SEQUENTIAL) on any columns — iter 3 showed these regress performance. Do NOT add an OMP warmup region — iter 3 showed this regresses. Keep lineitem columns as plain mmap with no madvise calls.
